papers.title_en: Citation-Aware Continual Pre-Training for Biomedical Language Models
papers.tldr: 本研究针对生物医学语言模型预训练中忽略文献引用关系的问题，提出了一种引用感知的持续预训练方法。该方法通过整合文献间的引用链接这一结构化知识，使模型能够更好地捕捉科学研究之间的关联。实验证明，这种方法显著增强了模型对生物医学领域知识的理解，为构建更强大的领域专用语言模型提供了新思路。
papers.abstract_en: The biomedical literature contains rich structured knowledge, including citation linksthat encode relationships between scientific studies, but such information is typicallyignored in standard language model pre-training. We propose a citation-aware …

papers.title_en: MME-Reasoning: A Broad-Spectrum Benchmark for Evaluating Logical Reasoning in MLLMs
papers.tldr: 本研究针对现有基准测试在评估多模态大语言模型（MLLM）逻辑推理能力方面的不足，提出了 MME-Reasoning。这是一个广谱基准测试，旨在全面衡量模型在处理多模态任务时的逻辑思维水平。通过该基准，研究者能够更深入地分析当前模型的推理瓶颈，为提升 MLLM 的智能水平提供了重要的评估工具和参考。
papers.abstract_en: Logical reasoning is a fundamental aspect of human intelligence and an essentialcapability for multimodal large language models (MLLMs). Despite the significantadvancement in multimodal reasoning, existing benchmarks fail to comprehensively …

papers.title_en: LoopCoder: Scaling Code Intelligence via Looped Language Models
papers.tldr: 针对大语言模型在复杂算法推理方面的局限性，本文提出了 LoopCoder。该模型借鉴了通用 Transformer 的思想，通过循环利用模型层（权重共享）来增强代码智能。LoopCoder 在不显著增加参数量的情况下，通过增加计算深度提升了模型的逻辑推理能力，为提升代码生成质量提供了一种高效且可扩展的路径。
papers.abstract_en: While large language models (LLMs) have mastered syntax-level code generation,complex algorithmic reasoning remains a challenge, typically addressed by scalingmodel depth and parameter count. Universal Transformers (UT) offer a compelling …

papers.title_en: Cached Summary Embeddings for Memory-Efficient EHR Inference
papers.tldr: 本研究针对Transformer临床预测模型在处理长病史时内存消耗过大的问题，提出了一种“缓存摘要嵌入”部署架构。该架构通过优化推理阶段的内存管理，使模型能在资源受限的环境下高效运行。研究证明该方法在显著降低内存需求的同时，保持了对电子健康记录的预测精度，为医疗AI的实际部署提供了技术支撑。
papers.abstract_en: Transformer-based clinical prediction models face a deployment challenge:processing long patient histories can require memory that exceeds availableresources in resourceconstrained settings. We propose a deployment architecture …

papers.title_en: Hierarchical spatial perception network and SAM-assisted uncertainty suppression for medical image segmentation
papers.tldr: 针对医学图像中解剖结构复杂多变导致的分割难题，本文提出了一种结合分层空间感知网络与SAM辅助不确定性抑制的新型分割框架。该方法通过分层提取空间特征，并利用Segment Anything Model (SAM) 的先验知识来辅助降低预测的不确定性，旨在显著提高临床引导手术中医学图像分割的准确性、鲁棒性及对复杂结构的适应能力。
papers.abstract_en: Accurate medical image segmentation is critical for image-guided clinicalprocedures, yet the complexity and variability of anatomical structures make it highlychallenging. To address this, we propose a novel segmentation framework that …

papers.title_en: FedFound: a federated foundation model for lifespan brain morphological connectome analysis
papers.tldr: 本研究提出了FedFound，这是一种针对全生命周期大脑形态连接组分析的联邦基础模型。该模型利用结构MRI衍生的形态连接组来表征个体差异并检测异常。通过联邦学习框架，FedFound能够在保护隐私的同时整合多中心数据，为跨年龄段的大脑发育和衰老研究提供了强有力的表征工具，在识别脑部异常方面表现出色。
papers.abstract_en: The brain morphological connectome derived from structural MRI reflects inter-regional morphological relationships, providing a powerful representation forcharacterizing individual variability and detecting abnormalities across the lifespan …

papers.title_en: Anatomy consistent segmentation network for joint PET/CT tumor segmentation
papers.tldr: 本研究针对PET/CT肿瘤分割中多模态特征纠缠导致性能受限的问题，提出了一种解剖一致性分割网络。该方法通过深度整合PET的功能代谢信息与CT的解剖结构信息，旨在解决模态间特征融合的难题。研究的主要贡献在于通过保持解剖一致性来增强特征提取的准确性，从而显著提升了临床肿瘤分割的性能和可靠性。
papers.abstract_en: Joint PET/CT imaging combines functional and anatomical information, makingmultimodal tumor segmentation essential in clinical oncology. However,segmentation performance is often hindered by inter-modal feature entanglement …

papers.tldr: 现代语言模型通常采用堆叠相同层的架构，导致参数在深度上均匀分布。本文挑战了这一传统设计，提出了“渐变式语言模型”（Tapered Language Models），通过在不同深度非均匀地分配参数来打破层级一致性的限制。研究表明，这种渐变式的参数布局能更有效地利用计算资源，在保持性能的同时显著提升模型效率，为优化大模型架构设计提供了重要参考。
papers.abstract_en: Modern language models, including transformer, recurrent, and memory-basedvariants, share a common chassis: a stack of identical layers in which parameters areallocated uniformly across depth. This is a default inherited from the original …

papers.title_en: Transition-Aware best-of-N sampling for Longitudinal Chest X-ray Reports
papers.tldr: 本研究针对纵向胸部X光报告生成，提出了一种无需训练的Transition-Aware best-of-N采样方法。在临床实践中，放射科医生通常结合患者之前的检查来描述病情变化。该方法通过在推理阶段优化采样策略，使生成的报告能更准确地反映两次检查间的动态演变，是首个针对该任务的免训练采样方案，有效提升了报告的临床相关性。
papers.abstract_en: In longitudinal clinical practice, every chest X-ray is read in the context of the patientsprior exam, and much of what the radiologist communicates is the change from onevisit to the next. To the best of our knowledge, we present the first training-free best-of …

papers.title_en: Seeing Through Multiple Views: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning via Selective Neurons for Consistent Radiology Report Generation
papers.tldr: 该研究针对放射科报告生成（RRG）中多视图X射线图像融合不充分的问题，提出了一种基于选择性神经元的参数高效微调（PEFT）方法。通过识别并利用对多视图一致性起关键作用的神经元，该方法在大幅减少可训练参数的同时，显著提升了生成报告的临床准确性和视图间的一致性，为多视图医学影像分析提供了高效且精准的新方案。
papers.abstract_en: Recent years have seen substantial advances in radiology report generation (RRG),yet existing approaches predominantly adopt direct feature fusion when handlingmulti-view X-ray images. Such approaches overlook the potential clinical …

papers.title_en: AtomiMed: Hierarchical Atomic Fact-Checking for Universal Clinical-Aware Medical Report Evaluation
papers.tldr: 针对医疗报告生成中传统指标（如n-gram）无法准确衡量临床事实准确性且易忽略严重诊断错误的问题，本文提出了AtomiMed。这是一种分层原子事实核查框架，通过将报告分解为原子事实并进行临床感知的层级化评估，为医疗报告提供更精确、符合临床逻辑的质量衡量标准，显著提升了评估的可靠性。
papers.abstract_en: Traditional metrics for Medical Report Generation (MRG) predominantly rely onsurface-level n-gram overlap, which fails to capture clinical factual accuracy andoften overlooks catastrophic diagnostic errors. We address this fundamental …

papers.title_en: AutoTrainess: Teaching Language Models to Improve Language Models Autonomously
papers.tldr: AutoTrainess 旨在解决语言模型训练过程中对人力的高度依赖问题。该研究提出了一种让语言模型自主改进其他语言模型的方法，利用先进模型在软件工程和长程任务中的能力，构建了一个自动化的训练闭环。这一方法显著降低了人工干预，为实现自我进化的 AI 系统提供了新路径。
papers.abstract_en: Training language models (LMs) remains a highly human-intensive process, even asfrontier language model agents become increasingly capable at softwareengineering and other long-horizon tasks. A central challenge is that autonomous …

papers.title_en: Token-Sparse Medical Multimodal Reasoning via Dual-Stream Reinforcement Learning
papers.tldr: 本研究针对医疗多模态推理中视觉证据极度稀疏的挑战，提出了一种基于双流强化学习（Dual-Stream RL）的视觉语言模型优化方法。该方法通过双流架构有效捕捉稀疏的关键临床信息，提升了模型在复杂医疗影像下的推理能力，在多项医疗多模态任务中取得了显著进展，为临床决策提供了更精准的支持。
papers.abstract_en: Vision-language models (VLMs) combining reinforcement learning (RL) igniteremarkable progress in multimodal reasoning, yet still struggle with medical images,which typically exhibit extremely sparse visual evidence to inform clinical decision …

papers.title_en: Breaking Failure Cascades: Step-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Medical Multimodal Reasoning
papers.tldr: 本研究针对医学多模态大模型在临床影像推理中存在的“级联失效”问题，指出传统的以结果为导向的训练忽略了中间步骤的正确性。为此，作者提出了一种步骤感知的强化学习方法，通过对推理链中的每个环节进行细粒度监督和优化，有效纠正了逻辑偏差。实验证明，该方法显著提升了模型在复杂医学推理任务中的表现，为构建更可靠的医疗辅助诊断系统提供了新思路。
papers.abstract_en: Recent multimodal large language models have shown great promise in clinicalimage reasoning, but existing post-training pipelines remain predominantly outcome-centric, relying on final answer correctness or sequence-level preferences. This …

papers.title_en: Towards Voxel Spacing Consistency for Medical Image Segmentation
papers.tldr: 本研究针对医学图像分割中物理体素间距不一致的问题，指出当前深度学习模型往往忽略了图像的实际物理尺度。文章提出了一种旨在实现体素间距一致性的新方法，通过在网络设计或数据处理中引入空间感知机制，增强了模型对不同分辨率数据的泛化能力。该方法在多个医学影像数据集上取得了显著的性能提升，为提高临床诊断和手术导航的精准度提供了重要支持。
papers.abstract_en: Volumetric medical image segmentation is essential for both preoperative diagnosisand intraoperative guidance. While recent years have witnessed rapid progress insegmentation architectures, comparatively little attention is paid to the physical voxel …

papers.title_en: Synergistic Perception-Reasoning Governance: Grounding Medical MLLMs with Verifiable Anatomical Evidence
papers.tldr: 本研究针对医疗多模态大模型（MLLM）在临床问答和影像报告生成中存在的幻觉问题，提出了“协同感知-推理治理”框架。该方法通过引入可验证的解剖学证据，将模型的推理过程与图像中的具体解剖区域挂钩，从而显著减少了生成内容与影像事实不符的情况，提升了医疗AI的可信度与准确性。
papers.abstract_en: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) show strong promise for clinical VQAand radiology report generation, yet inference-time hallucinations still underminetrustworthy use: models can produce fluent conclusions that conflict with imaging …

papers.title_en: Understanding Why Language Models Hallucinate: Testing Reasoning Against Priors
papers.tldr: 本研究探讨了大语言模型产生幻觉的深层原因，重点分析幻觉是源于知识缺失还是推理能力不足。通过设计实验测试模型在提示词约束与内部先验知识冲突时的表现，研究揭示了模型在处理矛盾信息时的决策机制。该工作为理解幻觉现象提供了新视角，区分了“不知道”与“推理错误”两种失效模式，对提升模型可靠性具有重要意义。
papers.abstract_en: Large language models often produce hallucinated answers that violate prompt-levelconstraints. A key diagnostic question is whether these failures reflect missingknowledge, or whether the model has the relevant information but follows the wrong …
